MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-15-2023 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR ORDER THAT MATTERS IN REQUEST FOR ADMISSION OF TRUTH OF FACTS BE ADMITTED IS HELD. MR. APPS DOES NOT STIPULATE. JPT KAHN STATED THAT THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT CONTESTED AND THAT THE PARTIES ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM HEARNG THE MOTION. COUNSEL STATED THAT THE MATTER HAS SETTLED. FOLLOWING BRIEF DISCUSSIONS, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE PRO TEM: MATTHEW KAHN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-15-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Stanley R. Apps, Esq. of the Law Offices of Robert S. Gitmeid P.C., 310.709.3966, for Defendant Brenda Mercado;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted.

Mr. Apps does not stipulate to the to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge.

Judge Pro Tem Kahn stated that the tentative ruling was not contested, that the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the judge pro tem hearng the motion, and that an order confirming the tentative ruling will be signed.

Mr. Apps stated that the matter has settled. Brief discussions are held. Judge Pro Tem Kahn stated that the proposed order could include additional language which states that the order may be vacated since the case was settled prior to entry of today's order.

Following discussions, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: The motion is GRANTED as unopposed. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(b), the truth of the matters specified in the requests is deemed admitted. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn at mkahn@gibsondunn.com. The order may include the language regarding the settlement and vacating the order as discussed today.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
